danger_signals.visual_form: Animated Goomba and Koopa Troopa sprites with distinct silhouettes and movement patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring evasion or counter-action via jumping

danger_signals.visual_form: Empty space/gap in the level terrain where the platform ends
danger_signals.meaning: Fatal fall hazard; represents instant death if player moves right into it

danger_signals.visual_form: Countdown timer displayed on HUD, decreasing visually
danger_signals.meaning: World state under urgency; level failure if timer reaches zero

danger_signals.visual_form: Reduced sprite size when power-up has been lost
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability: next hit will result in death rather than size reduction

danger_signals.visual_form: Solid brown brick blocks forming walls and platforms
danger_signals.meaning: Barriers requiring power-ups to break through or navigation around

safety_signals.visual_form: Brick-textured block with ? symbol visually distinct from solid bricks
safety_signals.meaning: Hidden resource container; hitting yields coins or power-ups for progression aid

safety_signals.visual_form: Red mushroom sprite with distinct shape emerging from questioned brick
safety_signals.meaning: Power-up that doubles Mario's size and grants damage absorption

safety_signals.visual_form: Animated flower sprite in contrasting red/yellow
safety_signals.meaning: Offensive power-up enabling ranged attacks against enemies

safety_signals.visual_form: Animated star sprite with distinct sparkling visual effect
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invincibility state; enemies can be defeated by direct contact

safety_signals.visual_form: Green spotted mushroom visually distinct from red Super Mushroom
safety_signals.meaning: Extra life resource; hidden reward for exploration or performance

safety_signals.visual_form: Small spinning coin sprites scattered throughout level
safety_signals.meaning: Points/score indicator; 100 coins grants extra life (safety resource)

safety_signals.visual_form: Continuous brown brick or green grass terrain under player
safety_signals.meaning: Safe traversal surface; enables forward progression

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mario's size and position on screen convey player agency; growth via mushroom shows faction strength increase

faction_indicators.color_identity: Dark/black colored enemy sprites (Koopa Troopas, Bowser)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Koopa Troopa (regular enemy with shell), Bowser (boss character, larger scaled sprite)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle setting in later worlds; enemy density increases toward final fortress; lava/darker terrain in Bowser's domain

faction_indicators.color_identity: Green grass, blue sky, brown brick architecture
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Grassland terrain, pipes, platforms; visual progression from natural landscape (early worlds) to castle architecture (later worlds)
Flagpole at end of each level serves as visual objective marker; castle structure indicates princess location

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Daylit grassland with clear platforms, moderate enemy density, blue sky background
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard enemy patrol patterns; Mario moves right unobstructed except for hazards

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Darker color palette shift; castle structures replace grassland; increased enemy density; lava/hazard terrain appears
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy patterns become more aggressive; pit hazards increase; level layout grows more complex

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Black background with brick-only architecture; no sky visible; coins/blocks more densely packed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enclosed spaces forcing specific navigation paths; different enemy placement patterns than overworld

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Castle interior setting with darker stone textures; Bowser sprite visible on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bowser executes attack patterns (fireballs, jumping); requires specific counter-strategy beyond standard enemy avoidance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Flagpole sprite at level end; visual transition/fade when player reaches it
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Screen locks; level clears; world/level counter increments; player spawns at next stage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Timer display flashing or changing color as countdown nears zero
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level fails immediately if timer reaches zero; player movement must accelerate or risk timeout death

environmental_storytelling.example: Early worlds feature natural grassland with simple platforms; middle worlds introduce castle-like structures; final worlds are entirely castle/fortress interior, telegraphing approach to villain's domain
Worlds 1-2 have sparse, predictable enemy patterns (single Goombas, isolated Koopas); later worlds cluster multiple enemies and force complex navigation, indicating rising opposition strength
Pits appear first as small gaps in early levels; grow wider in middle sections; become complex obstacle gauntlets in later worlds, showing escalating danger
Black background with brick-only terrain vs. blue sky grassland; visual cue signals alternate path or secret area without explicit text
Cool blue/green palette in early worlds transitions to darker reds/blacks in Bowser's castle, suggesting heat/danger and villain's stronghold
Flagpole is always visible or quickly accessible; player learns it signals level completion and safety through repeated encounters

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Walks left-right in predictable pattern; bounces when encountering platform edge; visual animation cycles continuously
No evasion behavior; Mario jumping on it flattens sprite instantly (defeated state)
Walks in directional pattern; turns at platform edges; sprite shows shell and legs clearly
Retracts into shell; shell becomes projectile usable by player; original enemy sprite disappears into shell sprite
Fires fireballs in arcing patterns; jumps across screen at intervals; larger sprite signals boss-level threat; castle setting frames confrontation
Single-height sprite; can fit through small gaps; cannot break brick blocks above; one hit results in death
Double-height sprite; can break brick blocks above by jumping; one hit returns to small state (damage absorption visual)
Sprite shows flower; pressing attack button fires projectile fireballs in forward direction; enemy sprites show defeat when hit by fire
Sprite flashes or shows invincibility aura; enemies defeated on contact; temporary state with visual countdown timer effect

notes: Super Mario Bros. uses minimal narrative text; world-state communication relies entirely on visual design, sprite behavior, and environmental architecture. The game establishes a 'visual grammar' where color, size, density, and environmental setting cumulatively signal danger, progression, and objective. No faction dialogue or political conflict exists; antagonism is purely environmental/mechanical. The tutorial nature of World 1-1 demonstrates this visual communication system clearly: single enemies, clear platforms, no pits initially. All threat escalation and world-state shifts are conveyed through incremental visual changes rather than explicit UI or dialogue.
